text:
CT SCAN - CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, status post right radical nephrectomy and retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. Currently on cabozantinib 40 mg daily (dose-reduced from 60 mg for hand-foot skin reaction) as second-line therapy following progression on Yervoy/nivo. Restaging examination at approximately 9 months of cabozantinib therapy (cumulative approximately 18 months from original diagnosis). Comparison to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis from approximately 3 months ago and original baseline.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed with intravenous administration of nonionic iodinated contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL via right antecubital vein). Acquisitions obtained in portal venous phase (abdomen/pelvis) and in delayed reconstructed phase as clinically appropriate. Reconstructions performed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes at 3 mm and 1.25 mm slice thickness. Lung windows reviewed for pulmonary nodule assessment. DLP: 1,248 mGy·cm.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (3 months prior, cabozantinib 6-month scan); CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (cabozantinib initiation baseline, approximately 9 months prior); original staging CT (approximately 18 months prior).

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally. No endobronchial lesion identified.

Pulmonary nodules: There are several bilateral pulmonary nodules, all decreased or unchanged compared to prior examination.

- Right lower lobe dominant nodule (previously the largest target lesion): The previously described right lower lobe nodule now measures approximately 2.0 cm in greatest dimension (axial series 4, image 143), compared to 2.1 cm on the prior study three months ago, and 2.6 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation. This represents continued slow but incremental interval reduction. The nodule remains solid and well-marginated, without internal cavitation or satellite lesions. Compared to original pre-treatment baseline of 1.8 cm, this lesion showed initial growth followed by gradual regression with cabozantinib.

- Right lower lobe second nodule: Measures approximately 0.4 cm, grossly stable compared to 0.4 cm on the prior examination and 0.5 cm at cabozantinib baseline.

- Left lower lobe nodule: Now measures approximately 0.3 cm, minimally decreased from 0.4 cm on the prior scan. This is a subtle change at the limit of measurement reproducibility.

- Right middle lobe nodules: Two previously described right middle lobe nodules (first identified at the time of progression on immunotherapy) now measure approximately 0.3 cm and 0.2 cm, compared to 0.3 cm and 0.2 cm on the prior examination. Both are at or below the threshold of reliable measurement and are considered stable to minimally decreased. No new right middle lobe nodules identified.

- No new pulmonary nodules are identified in either lung. Previously described additional subcentimeter nodules in the right and left lower lobes are below reliable measurement threshold and not individually measurable on this examination; none appear to have grown.

No pulmonary consolidation. No ground-glass opacity. No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax. No pericardial effusion.

Mediastinum: The mediastinal contour is within normal limits. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. Cardiac silhouette is normal in size. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. No pericardial thickening.

Chest Wall / Soft Tissues: No chest wall lesion. No axillary adenopathy.

---

ABDOMEN:

Post-surgical anatomy: Status post right radical nephrectomy and retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. The surgical bed is well-healed with expected postoperative fibrosis in the right retroperitoneum. No discrete fluid collection or hematoma at the nephrectomy bed. No local tumor recurrence identified.

Liver: The liver demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal lesion, mass, or hypervascular nodule. No hepatic cyst. No biliary dilatation. Portal vein patent.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesion.

Pancreas: Unremarkable. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilatation.

Adrenal glands: The left adrenal gland is normal in size and morphology. The right adrenal gland was resected en bloc at the time of nephrectomy; no evidence of adrenal bed recurrence.

Left kidney: The solitary left kidney is morphologically normal with symmetric parenchymal enhancement and prompt excretion of contrast into the collecting system. No hydronephrosis. No left renal mass. Compensatory mild left renal hypertrophy is again noted.

Retroperitoneal lymph nodes: The previously described right paracaval lymph node, which measured 1.1 cm at the time of progression on immunotherapy and had decreased to 0.6 cm on the 6-month cabozantinib scan, is no longer discretely measurable on this examination. In its expected location, there is a small linear soft tissue density measuring less than 4 mm, most consistent with residual fibrosis or a benign lymph node. No other retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No new para-aortic nodal disease.

Bowel: Small and large bowel loops are normal in caliber and wall thickness. No obstruction. No free air. No mesenteric stranding or inflammatory change.

Peritoneum/Ascites: No ascites. No peritoneal implants or omental nodularity identified.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: Moderately distended and unremarkable. No intraluminal mass. No bladder wall thickening.

Uterus and Ovaries: The uterus is mildly atrophic, consistent with postmenopausal status. No pelvic sidewall mass or adenopathy. No adnexal lesion.

Bones and Soft Tissues: No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

---

SKELETAL SURVEY (as imaged):

No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion suspicious for metastatic disease is identified within the imaged axial skeleton, including the thoracic and lumbar spine, ribs, sternum, and pelvis. Multilevel thoracic and lumbar spondylosis noted, consistent with the patient's known degenerative disease. No pathological compression fractures. No aggressive periosteal reaction. Note: MRI lumbar spine performed separately (approximately 6 weeks prior) showed only degenerative changes without osseous metastatic disease.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Continued slow interval decrease in the right lower lobe dominant pulmonary metastasis, now measuring approximately 2.0 cm compared to 2.1 cm three months ago and 2.6 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation. The trajectory of gradual reduction has been maintained over the 9-month treatment course.

2. The remaining bilateral pulmonary nodules are grossly stable to minimally decreased, with no new pulmonary nodules identified.

3. The previously noted right paracaval retroperitoneal lymph node is no longer discretely measurable, representing what appears to be a near-complete response of this site. Only a small sub-centimeter soft tissue density remains in this location, likely reflecting residual fibrosis.

4. No new sites of metastatic disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis. No hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or peritoneal metastases.

5. No local recurrence at the nephrectomy bed.

6. The solitary left kidney appears structurally intact with normal enhancement and excretion.

Overall, these findings are consistent with sustained disease control on current therapy, with no evidence of interval progression.

Electronically signed by: Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging Division
Dictated and finalized.
